---
title: "North American Bat Monitoring Program in Mount Revelstoke and Glacier National Parks"
subtitle: "Manual Verification Data Summary 2025"
resources:
  - Logos/
format:
  html:
    grid:
      margin-width: 300px
    title-block-banner: "#1d4566"
    title-block-banner-color: "#F4F4F5"
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-expand: true
    toc-location: left
    css: styles.css
  pdf:
    tbl-pos: "H"
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
    papersize: letter
    extra_dependencies: ["float"]
    title-graphic: "Images/photos-20250609-193136.jpg"
    include-before-body:
      text: |
        \clearpage
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{placeins}
        \usepackage{float}
date: last-modified
author: 
  - name: "Camila Hurtado"
    affiliation: "NNW Bat Hub Coordinator"
  - name: "Brooke Daly"
    affiliation: "Manual Vetter"
editor: visual
bibliography: NABat-NNWHub-references.bib
nocite: '@*'
---

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
![Deployment at Rock Garden Slide Site at in the Glacier grid cell (GRTS ID: 271731) in 2025](Images/photos-20250609-193136.jpg)
:::

```{r}
#| label: Load packages and data
#| include: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
 
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
library(kableExtra)
library(tidyverse)
library(leaflet)
library(base64enc)
library(mapview)
library(sf)

MR <- read.csv("Data/241002_Mnt.Revelstoke.csv")
G <- read.csv("Data/271731_Glacier.csv")

MRG <- bind_rows(MR,G)

MRG$Date <- as.Date(MRG$Timestamp)
MRG$Time <- format(as.POSIXct(MRG$Timestamp, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "%H:%M:%S")

sites <- read.csv("Data/Sites.csv")

```

```{r}
```


```{r}
#| label: Create final species auto ID
#| include: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# --- Helper: Convert 6-letter Kaleidoscope code to 4-letter code ---
kal_to_4letter <- function(code) {
  code <- trimws(code)
  ifelse(
    grepl("^[A-Za-z]{6}$", code),
    paste0(substr(code, 1, 2), substr(code, 4, 5)),
    code
  )
}

# --- Helpers: Separate NOISE, NoID, and blank ---
is_blank <- function(x) {
  trimws(x) == "" | is.na(x)
}

is_no_id <- function(x) {
  trimws(toupper(x)) %in% c("NOID", "NA") & !is_blank(x)
}

is_noise <- function(x) {
  trimws(toupper(x)) == "NOISE"
}

is_unidentified <- function(x) {
  is_blank(x) | is_no_id(x) | is_noise(x)
}

# --- Species group codes (uppercase for consistent matching) ---
# These are group-level IDs, not resolved to species
sb_species_groups_upper <- toupper(c("Hif", "Hilo", "Lof"))

is_sb_group <- function(x) {
  toupper(trimws(x)) %in% sb_species_groups_upper
}

# ============================================================
# Main logic: build Final.Spp.Auto.ID
# ============================================================
MRG <- MRG %>%
  mutate(
    
    # Step 1: Convert Kaleidoscope to 4-letter code (uppercase)
    KAL.upper = toupper(kal_to_4letter(trimws(WA.Kaleidoscope.Auto.ID))),
    
    # Step 2: Standardize SB code to uppercase
    SB.upper = toupper(trimws(SB.Species.Auto.ID.verbose)),
    
    # Step 3: Apply priority rules
    Final.Spp.Auto.ID = case_when(
      
      # Both blank → leave empty
      is_blank(KAL.upper) & is_blank(SB.upper) ~ "",
      
      # One blank → use whatever the other column has
      is_blank(KAL.upper) & !is_blank(SB.upper) ~ SB.upper,
      !is_blank(KAL.upper) & is_blank(SB.upper) ~ KAL.upper,
      
      # Both are NoID → NoID
      is_no_id(KAL.upper) & is_no_id(SB.upper) ~ "NoID",
      
      # Both are NOISE → NOISE
      is_noise(KAL.upper) & is_noise(SB.upper) ~ "NOISE",
      
      # Either is NOISE and other is NoID → default to NoID
      is_noise(KAL.upper) & is_no_id(SB.upper) ~ "NoID",
      is_no_id(KAL.upper) & is_noise(SB.upper) ~ "NoID",
      
      # SB is a species GROUP and KAL has a species ID → use KAL species
      # (must come BEFORE the disagreement concat rule)
      is_sb_group(SB.upper) & !is_unidentified(KAL.upper) ~ KAL.upper,
      
      # SB is a species GROUP and KAL is unidentified → use SB group
      is_sb_group(SB.upper) & is_unidentified(KAL.upper) ~ SB.upper,
      
      # KAL is unidentified but SB has a resolved species → use SB
      is_unidentified(KAL.upper) & !is_unidentified(SB.upper) ~ SB.upper,
      
      # SB is unidentified but KAL has a resolved species → use KAL
      !is_unidentified(KAL.upper) & is_unidentified(SB.upper) ~ KAL.upper,
      
      # Both agree → use the 4-letter code
      KAL.upper == SB.upper ~ KAL.upper,
      
      # Both have different resolved species → concatenate
      !is_unidentified(KAL.upper) & !is_unidentified(SB.upper) & KAL.upper != SB.upper ~
        paste0(pmin(KAL.upper, SB.upper), pmax(KAL.upper, SB.upper)),
      
      # Fallback
      TRUE ~ "NoID"
    )
    
  ) %>%
  select(-KAL.upper, -SB.upper)

```

```{r}
#| label: Get list of sites, transects, unique IDs
#| include: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

site_dates <- MRG %>%
  subset(Quadrant!="Transects")%>%
  group_by(Quadrant)%>%
  summarize(
    earliest_date=min(Date, na.rm=TRUE),
    latest_date=max(Date, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_days= as.integer(latest_date-earliest_date)+1, 
    #Format dates to mm-dd
    earliest_date = format(earliest_date, "%B-%d"),
    latest_date = format(latest_date, "%B-%d")
  ) %>%
  # Sort by site name
  arrange(Quadrant)

transect_dates <- MRG %>%
  subset(Quadrant="Transects")%>%
  group_by(Quadrant)%>%
  summarize(
    earliest_date=min(Date, na.rm=TRUE),
    latest_date=max(Date, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_days= as.integer(latest_date-earliest_date)+1, 
    #Format dates to mm-dd
    earliest_date = format(earliest_date, "%B-%d"),
    latest_date = format(latest_date, "%B-%d")
  ) %>%
  # Sort by site name
  arrange(Quadrant)

```

```{r}
#| label: Prep data for map
#| include: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# ── 1. Summarise species per site ─────────────────────────────────────────────
exclude_ids <- c("Noise", "NoID", "NOISE", "NOID", "")

species_by_site <- MRG %>%
  filter(!Final.Spp.Auto.ID %in% exclude_ids,
         !is.na(Final.Spp.Auto.ID)) %>%
  mutate(
    GRTS_Quad = paste0(GRTS.Cell.ID, Quadrant),
    species_clean = case_when(
      nchar(Final.Spp.Auto.ID) == 8          ~ "Other",  # couplets
      Final.Spp.Auto.ID %in% c("HILO", "HIF", "LOF") ~ "Other",  # species groups
      .default = Final.Spp.Auto.ID
    )
  ) %>%
  count(GRTS_Quad, species_clean, name = "n") %>%
  rename(species = species_clean)

# ── 2. Join with site coordinates ─────────────────────────────────────────────
sites_coords <- sites %>%
  select(GRTS_Quad = Grid.Cell.and.Quadrant,
         site_name = Site.Quadrant.Name,
         lat = y, lon = x)

map_data <- species_by_site %>%
  left_join(sites_coords, by = "GRTS_Quad") %>%
  filter(!is.na(lat))
# ── 3. Colour palette ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SVG pie chart helper 
make_pie_svg <- function(values, colors, size = 70) {
  total <- sum(values)
  if (total == 0) return(NULL)
  fracs  <- values / total
  angles <- cumsum(c(0, fracs)) * 2 * pi
  r <- size / 2 - 2

  slices <- purrr::map2_chr(seq_along(values), colors, function(i, col) {
    a0 <- angles[i]; a1 <- angles[i + 1]
    if (abs(a1 - a0) >= 2 * pi - 1e-6) {
      sprintf('<circle cx="%s" cy="%s" r="%s" fill="%s"/>',
              size/2, size/2, r, col)
    } else {
      x0 <- size/2 + r*sin(a0); y0 <- size/2 - r*cos(a0)
      x1 <- size/2 + r*sin(a1); y1 <- size/2 - r*cos(a1)
      large <- if ((a1 - a0) > pi) 1L else 0L
      sprintf('<path d="M %s %s L %s %s A %s %s 0 %s 1 %s %s Z" fill="%s" stroke="white" stroke-width="1"/>',
              size/2, size/2, x0, y0, r, r, large, x1, y1, col)
    }
  })

  svg <- sprintf('<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="%s" height="%s">%s</svg>',
                 size, size, paste(slices, collapse = ""))
  paste0("data:image/svg+xml;base64,", base64enc::base64encode(charToRaw(svg)))
}
#this is based ont he spp that appeared the most. Need to update based on needs
sp_colors <- c(
  "EPFU" = "#F28E2B",   # orange
  "LANO" = "#E15759",   # red
  "MYEV" = "#4E79A7",   # blue
  "MYLU" = "#59A14F",   # green
  "MYVO" = "#9B59B6",   # purple
  "Other" = "#AAAAAA"   # grey
)
# ── 5. Build markers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
site_list <- map_data %>%
  group_by(GRTS_Quad, site_name, lat, lon) %>%
  summarise(sp_data = list(tibble(species, n)), total = sum(n), .groups = "drop")

# ── 6. Load other shapefiles───────────────────────────────────────────────────
#load shp for grid cell 
grids <- st_read("Mapping/MRG-NABatGrids.shp") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 4326) #transform to WGS84

transect <- st_read("Mapping/Transect.shp") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 4326) %>% #transform to WGS84
  st_zm(drop = TRUE, what = "ZM") %>% #drop the Z dimension so it renders
  select(geometry)
# ── 7. Draw map ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
m <- leaflet() %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>%
  addPolygons(
    data = grids,
    color = "#444444",
    weight = 1.5,
    fillColor = "#6baed6",
    fillOpacity = 0.3
  ) %>%
  addPolylines(
    data = transect,
    color = "#000072",
    weight = 2
  )

all_pie_colors <- c()  # collect colours as we loop

for (i in seq_len(nrow(site_list))) {
  row <- site_list[i, ]
  sp  <- row$sp_data[[1]] %>% arrange(desc(n))

  sp_full <- sp

  top4 <- sp$species[1:min(4, nrow(sp))]
  
  sp_pie <- sp %>%
    mutate(species_pie = if_else(species %in% top4, species, "Other")) %>%
    group_by(species_pie) %>%
    summarise(n = sum(n), .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(desc(n)) %>%
    rename(species = species_pie)
  #incorporate spp colours
  unknown_sp <- setdiff(sp_pie$species, names(sp_colors))
   fallback_cols <- setNames(
    colorRampPalette(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"))(max(length(unknown_sp), 1)),
    unknown_sp)
  pie_colors <- c(sp_colors, fallback_cols)[sp_pie$species]
  pie_colors <- setNames(pie_colors, sp_pie$species)  
  # Accumulate colours across sites
  all_pie_colors <- c(all_pie_colors, pie_colors)
  uri <- make_pie_svg(sp_pie$n, pie_colors[sp_pie$species])
  popup_rows <- sp_full %>%
    mutate(pct = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1)) %>%
    purrr::pmap_chr(function(species, n, pct) sprintf(
      '<tr><td><span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;margin-right:4px"></span>%s</td><td align="right">%s</td><td align="right">%s%%</td></tr>',
      ifelse(species %in% names(pie_colors), pie_colors[species], "#AAAAAA"),
      species, n, pct))
  popup <- sprintf(
    '<div style="font-family:sans-serif;min-width:180px"><b>%s</b><br/><small>%s</small><hr style="margin:5px 0"/><b>%s</b> detections<table style="width:100%%;margin-top:4px"><tr><th align="left">Species</th><th align="right">n</th><th align="right">%%</th></tr>%s</table></div>',
    row$site_name, row$GRTS_Quad, row$total,
    paste(popup_rows, collapse = ""))
  m <- m %>% addMarkers(
    lng  = row$lon, lat = row$lat,
    icon = makeIcon(iconUrl = uri, iconWidth = 70, iconHeight = 70,
                    iconAnchorX = 35, iconAnchorY = 35),
    popup = popup,
    label = row$site_name
  )
}

# ── 7. Legend HTML (built after loop so we know which species appear) ─────────
# Deduplicate: keep first occurrence of each species name
legend_colors <- all_pie_colors[!duplicated(names(all_pie_colors))]
legend_colors <- legend_colors[order(names(legend_colors))]  # alphabetical
if ("Other" %in% names(legend_colors)) {
  # Move "Other" to the end
  legend_colors <- c(legend_colors[names(legend_colors) != "Other"],
                     legend_colors["Other"])
}

legend_html <- htmltools::tags$div(
  style = "background:white;padding:10px 14px;border-radius:6px;box-shadow:0 1px 5px rgba(0,0,0,.3);font-family:sans-serif;font-size:12px;max-height:300px;overflow-y:auto;",
  htmltools::tags$b("Species"),
  htmltools::tags$hr(style = "margin:4px 0"),
  htmltools::HTML(paste(purrr::map_chr(names(legend_colors), ~sprintf(
    '<div style="display:flex;align-items:center;margin-bottom:3px"><span style="display:inline-block;width:12px;height:12px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;margin-right:6px"></span>%s</div>',
    legend_colors[.x], .x)), collapse = ""))
)

m <- m %>% addControl(legend_html, position = "bottomright")

```

# Executive Summary

# Land Acknowledgement

Biodiversity Pathways respectfully acknowledges that our work takes place on Treaty 8 and Douglas Treaties Territories as well as the traditional and unceded territories of First Nations and Métis Peoples across all regions of British Columbia, whose histories, languages, and cultures are deeply connected to the biodiversity we monitor. We acknowledge the traditional teachings of the lands that we work on, and that reciprocal, meaningful, and respectful relationships with Indigenous peoples make our work possible. We are deeply grateful for their stewardship of these lands, and we are committed to supporting Indigenous-led monitoring programs, while learning Indigenous ways of knowing, being, and doing.

# Introduction

# Methodology

# Results

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| fig-cap: Proportion of species bat passes at each site. Other represents tags that were identified as species couplets or species groups
#| label: fig-monitoring-locations

if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  m
} else {
  pad <- 0.01
  
  bbox_list <- list(
    list(
      lng1 = -118.2136 + pad, lat1 = 50.95249 + pad,
      lng2 = -118.0392 - pad, lat2 = 51.06404 - pad,
      title = "Mt. Revelstoke Sites"
    ),
    list(
      lng1 = -117.7420 + pad, lat1 = 51.22348 + pad,
      lng2 = -117.5671 - pad, lat2 = 51.33451 - pad,
      title = "Glacier Sites"
    )
  )
  
  dir.create(file.path(getwd(), "Figures"), showWarnings = FALSE)
  
  for (bbox in bbox_list) {
    map_file <- file.path(getwd(), "Figures", paste0(gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", bbox$title), ".png"))
    
    m_zoomed <- m %>%
      fitBounds(
        lng1 = bbox$lng1, lat1 = bbox$lat1,
        lng2 = bbox$lng2, lat2 = bbox$lat2
      ) %>%
      addControl(legend_html, position = "bottomright")
    
    tryCatch({
      mapview::mapshot2(
        m_zoomed,
        file = map_file,
        delay = 5,
        vwidth = 1200,
        vheight = 800
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      message("mapshot2 failed for ", bbox$title, ": ", e$message)
    })
  }
  
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
![Sampled sites in Glacier National Park (GRTS 271731). Proportion of species bat passes at each site. Other represents tags that were identified as species couplets or species groups](Figures/Glacier_Sites.png){#fig-glacier}

![Sampled sites and driving transect route in Mount Revelstoke National Park (GRTS 241002). Proportion of species bat passes at each site. Other represents tags that were identified as species couplets or species groups](Figures/Mt__Revelstoke_Sites.png)
:::

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| tbl-cap: Species confirmed via manual verification at Mount Revelstoke and Glacier National Parks

MRG$Species.Manual.ID

```

# Discussion
